symptoms.symptom_name: Solitary oval scaly herald patch ~10 days ago, then an explosive secondary eruption of many smaller oval trunk lesions over 24-48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A herald patch preceding a generalised crop of smaller oval lesions by days to two weeks is the defining biphasic time-course of pityriasis rosea.
Tinea does not produce a sudden explosive generalised crop of hundreds of lesions from a single patch over 24-48 hours.

symptoms.symptom_name: Small oval salmon-coloured papules/plaques with a fine inward-facing (trailing) collarette of scale, long axes following skin cleavage lines ('Christmas tree')

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Oval salmon plaques with an inward-facing collarette of scale distributed along the cleavage lines are near-defining for pityriasis rosea.
Tinea scale is outward-facing on the advancing leading edge, the opposite of the inward-facing collarette seen here.
Guttate psoriasis has thick adherent silvery scale and lacks a collarette and a cleavage-line orientation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Moderate pruritus worse with heat/sweating; systemically well; severe health anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild-to-moderate itch worse with heat is typical of pityriasis rosea but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Face, palms and soles spared; no mucosal patches; no generalised lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secondary syphilis classically involves the palms and soles with mucosal patches and lymphadenopathy; their absence argues strongly against it, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target telehealth cannot serologically exclude.
Sparing of the face, palms and soles fits the typical truncal distribution of pityriasis rosea.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile and systemically well; no blistering, skin peeling, skin pain or mucosal sores

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe cutaneous adverse drug reaction / drug eruption
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, mucosal involvement, blistering, skin peeling and skin pain argues strongly against SJS/TEN or another severe cutaneous reaction, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new prescription/OTC medication, antibiotic or supplement in the preceding weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe cutaneous adverse drug reaction / drug eruption
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of any new culprit drug argues strongly against a drug eruption / severe cutaneous adverse drug reaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild viral prodrome (fatigue, sore throat) ~10 days before the eruption

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A preceding mild viral prodrome fits the HHV-6/7-associated pathogenesis of pityriasis rosea and its typical herald-patch timing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine trailing (not thick/adherent/silvery) scale; no preceding streptococcal tonsillitis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Guttate psoriasis has thick adherent silvery scale and usually follows a streptococcal throat infection — neither is present here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 122/78, HR 85, Temp 36.9, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 68 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe cutaneous adverse drug reaction / drug eruption
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement; normal, afebrile and haemodynamically stable vitals argue against a systemic or severe cutaneous reaction. Not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine GP review (in person or telehealth) to confirm pityriasis rosea and reassess if atypical or not resolving
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct inspection of the herald patch and collarette scale and palpation for lymphadenopathy are the assessments telehealth cannot fully do; review confirms the benign diagnosis and picks up an atypical course.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Syphilis serology (RPR/TPPA) if the palms/soles or mucosa become involved, if lymphadenopathy appears, or if the eruption is otherwise atypical
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for a classic pityriasis rosea with clear palms/soles and no mucosal/lymph-node involvement; reserve it for atypical or palm/sole-involving presentations, where secondary syphilis must be excluded.
Not indicated — the collarette scale, herald patch and cleavage-line distribution are characteristic of pityriasis rosea, not tinea; scraping is over-investigation here.
Not indicated for a classic benign self-limiting eruption; would be over-investigation of a clinical diagnosis.
